meta.name: The Ashen Sovereignty of Aethelgard
meta.tagline: A kingdom of ghosts defending a throne that no longer exists.

aesthetic.visualStyle: Faded gold leaf peeling from blackened stone, mist clinging to bone-white forests, twilight that lasts for weeks. Colors are desaturated: slate, charcoal, pale violet, and rust.
inspirations: The final days of Rome in art

archetype:ranger-kings-shadow.name: Ranger-King’s Shadow
archetype:ranger-kings-shadow.tagline: Navigate the past to strike the present.
archetype:ranger-kings-shadow.description: As a reaction when tracking or setting an ambush, spend 1 memory token to treat difficult terrain as easy ground, or add +2d6 damage to your next attack. The environment briefly shifts to its ancient, living state, granting you perfect footing and silent movement.

features.name: Echoes of Green
features.description: As a reaction when tracking or setting an ambush, spend 1 memory token to treat difficult terrain as easy ground, or add +2d6 damage to your next attack. The environment briefly shifts to its ancient, living state, granting you perfect footing and silent movement.

archetype:ranger-kings-shadow.background: You carry a silver locket containing a pressed leaf from a forest that burned centuries ago; it still smells of rain when the ash falls thick. The King’s scouts still leave cairns on the high ridges; you know how to find them, but the stones are crumbling faster than the paths they mark.

archetype:lorekeeper-of-fading-things.name: Lorekeeper of Fading Things
archetype:lorekeeper-of-fading-things.tagline: Bind the past into power, pay with your own mind.
archetype:lorekeeper-of-fading-things.description: Touch an object, surface, or creature to extract a memory. You may spend this memory to grant an ally advantage on a skill check, or force an enemy to reroll a successful action based on what you’ve learned. Using it permanently erases one of your own recent memories from your mind.

features.name: Mnemosyne’s Tether
features.description: Touch an object, surface, or creature to extract a memory. You may spend this memory to grant an ally advantage on a skill check, or force an enemy to reroll a successful action based on what you’ve learned. Using it permanently erases one of your own recent memories from your mind.

archetype:lorekeeper-of-fading-things.background: You trade fragments of your childhood for translations of elven death-wards that still hum in the palace walls. You are secretly preserving the true names of the fallen, believing that as long as one voice speaks them aloud, they cannot truly fade into the ash.

archetype:oathsworn-of-the-empty-hall.name: Oathsworn of the Empty Hall
archetype:oathsworn-of-the-empty-hall.tagline: Hold the line when retreat means dishonor.
archetype:oathsworn-of-the-empty-hall.description: When you take damage that reduces you to half HP or below, you lock your posture and gain resistance to physical damage until the end of your next turn. Your attacks ignore armor class bonuses from cover or haste during this stance, representing unwavering duty over survival instinct.

features.name: Last Stand of the Hollow Court
features.description: When you take damage that reduces you to half HP or below, you lock your posture and gain resistance to physical damage until the end of your next turn. Your attacks ignore armor class bonuses from cover or haste during this stance, representing unwavering duty over survival instinct.

archetype:oathsworn-of-the-empty-hall.background: Your armor bears the dents of three hundred battles you were told were victories; you now suspect they were merely delays before the inevitable. You polish your shield every dawn out of habit, even though the royal standard was buried in the ash ten years ago and the hall beyond it is empty.
archetype:oathsworn-of-the-empty-hall.flavor: "We do not fight because we will win. We fight because we swore we would."

archetype:dragon-cultist-of-the-final-br.name: Dragon-Cultist of the Final Breath
archetype:dragon-cultist-of-the-final-br.tagline: Embrace the end as a gift, wield its warmth and ruin.
archetype:dragon-cultist-of-the-final-br.description: Exhale a cloud of sulfurous ash and ghostly light. Choose a 30-foot cone or sphere. Creatures must succeed on a CON save or be frightened and suffer disadvantage on attacks until the end of their next turn. Allies in the cloud gain temporary resistance to fire and cold, feeling the dragon’s ancient, peaceful warmth.

features.name: Breath of the Dying Sky
features.description: Exhale a cloud of sulfurous ash and ghostly light. Choose a 30-foot cone or sphere. Creatures must succeed on a CON save or be frightened and suffer disadvantage on attacks until the end of their next turn. Allies in the cloud gain temporary resistance to fire and cold, feeling the dragon’s ancient, peaceful warmth.

archetype:dragon-cultist-of-the-final-br.background: You collect shed dragon scales in a wax-sealed casket, pressing them to your chest like rosary beads while the court calls you heretic. You smile when the sky turns black and the wind carries the scent of ozone; you hear a lullaby in the storm that others mistake for screaming.
archetype:dragon-cultist-of-the-final-br.flavor: "The fire does not hate you. It only wishes to finish what the long night began."

item:sword-of-unending-rust.name: Sword of Unending Rust
item:sword-of-unending-rust.description: A longsword whose blade is perpetually flaking with orange-brown oxidation, yet never fully crumbles. The hilt is wrapped in faded silver thread that grips the palm like a dying hand. Deals 1d8 slashing damage. On a hit, target takes 1d4 bleed damage at the start of each of their turns until healed or cleaned. Once per long rest, can be driven into wooden structures or gates to rapidly rot them, bypassing normal hardness thresholds.
item:sword-of-unending-rust.flavor: "It does not cut flesh; it cuts time."

item:cloak-of-mist.name: Cloak of Mist
item:cloak-of-mist.description: Woven from gossamer threads that seem to drink the light around them. It hangs loosely, damp and cool to the touch, smelling of petrichor and old stone. Grants advantage on Stealth checks in fog, rain, or low light. When worn, allows one action per short rest to step into a nearby shadow or mist, appearing up to 30 ft away without provoking opportunity attacks.
item:cloak-of-mist.flavor: "Wear it, and the world forgets you were ever there."

item:vial-of-liquid-memory.name: Vial of Liquid Memory
item:vial-of-liquid-memory.description: A slender glass ampoule containing a swirling, iridescent fluid that shifts color with the viewer’s mood. The cork is sealed with wax stamped with a forgotten crest. Drink to regain 2d8 HP and immediately recall one piece of lost knowledge, a forgotten name, or a location previously missed. Lasts for 1 hour. Can only be used once per character.
item:vial-of-liquid-memory.flavor: "Some memories are too heavy to carry, so we pour them out and drink them instead."

consumable.effectDescription: Drink to regain 2d8 HP and immediately recall one piece of lost knowledge, a forgotten name, or a location previously missed. Lasts for 1 hour. Can only be used once per character.

item:compass-of-lost-places.name: Compass of Lost Places
item:compass-of-lost-places.description: A brass astrolabe-like device with three concentric rings etched with constellations that no longer exist. The needle trembles not toward north, but toward what has been erased. Points toward the nearest location tied to a significant loss or forgotten event. Requires a WIT check to interpret correctly; failure points to a mundane ruin instead.
item:compass-of-lost-places.flavor: "It does not guide you home. It guides you to what home used to be."

item:mask-of-scales.name: Mask of Scales
item:mask-of-scales.description: A fitted faceplate hammered from thin, overlapping plates of tarnished bronze, shaped like a serpent’s jaw. The eye slits are narrow, and the interior smells of dry earth and ozone. Grants resistance to fire damage. While wearing it, the wearer speaks in a layered, echoing voice that grants advantage on Persuasion/Intimidation checks against creatures of beast or draconic lineage.
item:mask-of-scales.flavor: "Breathe through it, and you smell the ash before it falls."

item:scroll-of-the-first-dawn.name: Scroll of the First Dawn
item:scroll-of-the-first-dawn.description: A brittle parchment the size of a shield, inscribed with gold leaf that catches light even in total darkness. The edges are singed, as if unrolled during a great conflagration. Contains a single, ancient spell or ritual that can restore a small area of blighted land or temporarily banish a minor shadow. Consumed upon casting.
item:scroll-of-the-first-dawn.flavor: "They say the first sunrise was written down before it happened."

item:shield-of-the-fallen-king.name: Shield of the Fallen King
item:shield-of-the-fallen-king.description: A kite shield carved from petrified heartwood, its surface scarred by countless blows. A single crack runs through the center, filled with hardened resin that glows faintly amber. Blocks one attack per round automatically. When raised, grants temporary HP equal to half the wielder’s CON modifier. Cracks deepen with each use, eventually requiring repair.
item:shield-of-the-fallen-king.flavor: "It held the line when the throne fell silent."

item:dagger-of-whispers.name: Dagger of Whispers
item:dagger-of-whispers.description: A stiletto forged from blackened steel, its blade thin as a razor and etched with tiny, spiraling runes. When drawn, it hums at a frequency just below hearing. Deals 1d4 piercing damage. Critical hits allow the wielder to hear the target’s last spoken word or thought, revealed as a whisper in their ear.
item:dagger-of-whispers.flavor: "Draw it close, and the dead will tell you how they died."

item:lantern-of-ghost-light.name: Lantern of Ghost Light
item:lantern-of-ghost-light.description: An iron caged lantern housing a flame that burns cold and pale blue. It casts long, sharp shadows that sometimes move independently of their source. Illuminates a 30-ft radius. Shadows within the light reveal hidden creatures or illusions. Once per day, can be extinguished to cast "Phantasmal Force" focused on fear or memory.
item:lantern-of-ghost-light.flavor: "It does not light the path. It shows you what is already walking it."

item:ring-of-forgetting.name: Ring of Forgetting
item:ring-of-forgetting.description: A simple band of oxidized copper, slightly too large for any finger. It feels warm when touched by someone who is trying hard to remember something important. While worn, the bearer can voluntarily erase one specific memory per day. That memory can be transferred to another creature via touch, who must succeed on a CON save or suffer disadvantage on related checks.
item:ring-of-forgetting.flavor: "Some burdens are meant to be passed, not carried."

item:boots-of-silent-step.name: Boots of Silent Step
item:boots-of-silent-step.description: Knee-high boots stitched from supple grey leather, soles wrapped in felt and bound with twine that never frays. They leave no prints, not even in fresh snow or mud. Grants advantage on DEX checks for movement-based actions. Allows walking across delicate surfaces (glass, spider silk, crumbling masonry) without triggering traps or breaking them.
item:boots-of-silent-step.flavor: "Tread lightly, for the ground remembers your weight."

item:amulet-of-ash-protection.name: Amulet of Ash Protection
item:amulet-of-ash-protection.description: A smooth river stone suspended on a braided cord of hair and wire. The stone is pitted and grey, warm to the touch, and constantly sheds a fine layer of ash onto whatever rests beneath it. Grants resistance to poison and necrotic damage. When worn during a short rest, clears one level of exhaustion caused by environmental hazards or lingering curses.
item:amulet-of-ash-protection.flavor: "The sky falls, but this stone holds the earth together."

item:quill-of-binding.name: Quill of Binding
item:quill-of-binding.description: A feather from a raven dyed indigo, tipped with a nib of polished obsidian. Ink flows from it without a bottle, drawn directly from the air around it. Used to write contracts or decrees that become magically binding. Any agreement written with it cannot be broken without a CON save or paying a physical toll (bleeding, scarring).
item:quill-of-binding.flavor: "Write your oath in it, and the words will haunt you until kept."

item:potion-of-eternal-vigilance.name: Potion of Eternal Vigilance
item:potion-of-eternal-vigilance.description: A thick, viscous liquid the color of tarnished silver, bottled in stoppered clay. It tastes of iron and dried sage. For 24 hours, the drinker gains advantage on initiative and perception checks, and cannot be surprised. Upon waking, they take 1d4 psychic damage as their mind refuses to fully rest.
item:potion-of-eternal-vigilance.flavor: "Sleep is a luxury for those who trust the dark."

consumable.effectDescription: For 24 hours, the drinker gains advantage on initiative and perception checks, and cannot be surprised. Upon waking, they take 1d4 psychic damage as their mind refuses to fully rest.

item:helm-of-the-dead-king.name: Helm of the Dead King
item:helm-of-the-dead-king.description: A dented coronet of dark iron, lined with faded crimson velvet. The interior bears a single, perfectly preserved tear-stain on the brow. Grants advantage on WIT checks to recall history or courtly etiquette. While worn, the bearer commands respect from commoners and lesser nobles, but dragons and ancient beings regard them with pity.
item:helm-of-the-dead-king.flavor: "He wore it until his crown became his cage."

item:map-of-shifting-lands.name: Map of Shifting Lands
item:map-of-shifting-lands.description: A parchment chart that slowly rewrites itself. Rivers change course, forests migrate, and cities appear and vanish depending on the reader’s belief. Provides accurate navigation through unstable terrain or planar bleeds. Requires a WIT check to stabilize a route; failure causes the map to show a plausible but incorrect path.
item:map-of-shifting-lands.flavor: "Trust it, and you’ll arrive somewhere entirely new."

item:chalice-of-bitter-wine.name: Chalice of Bitter Wine
item:chalice-of-bitter-wine.description: A goblet carved from a single piece of obsidian, rimmed with silver that has tarnished to black. It always contains a small amount of deeply red wine that never spoils. Sharing a drink from it establishes a bond of mutual understanding between two parties, granting advantage on persuasion checks between them for 1 hour. The wine tastes of sorrow.
item:chalice-of-bitter-wine.flavor: "Raise it to toast the past, and you will weep for what remains."

item:arrow-of-cold-fire.name: Arrow of Cold Fire
item:arrow-of-cold-fire.description: A shaft of pale ash wood fletched with white feathers, tipped with a crystal shard that pulses with a faint, freezing luminescence. It leaves a trail of frost in the air. Ranged weapon dealing 1d6 piercing + 1d6 cold damage. On hit, targets must succeed on a CON save or have their speed halved for one turn as ice crystallizes over their joints.
item:arrow-of-cold-fire.flavor: "It burns without heat, and freezes without winter."

item:tome-of-forgotten-tongues.name: Tome of Forgotten Tongues
item:tome-of-forgotten-tongues.description: A heavy codex bound in cracked leather, its pages made of pressed bark. The script shifts between dialects as the reader’s eyes scan it, translating itself into the reader’s native tongue. Grants proficiency in an ancient language. Allows one successful WIT check per day to decipher ruins, cast a minor ward, or understand the true names of spirits.
item:tome-of-forgotten-tongues.flavor: "Every word inside was spoken by someone who chose to be remembered."

item:key-to-the-sunken-library.name: Key to the Sunken Library
item:key-to-the-sunken-library.description: A heavy iron key with a bow shaped like a diving bell. The teeth are worn smooth, and it hums faintly when near water or buried archives. Unlocks submerged doors, vaults, or hidden compartments sealed with water magic. Once per session, can be used to exhale a pocket of breathable air underwater for 1 minute.
item:key-to-the-sunken-library.flavor: "It opens what the sea tried to keep."

monster:ash-golem.name: Ash Golem
monster:ash-golem.description: A hulking figure of compacted volcanic dust and jagged bone fragments, held together by the faint, dying hum of old magic. It leaves a trail of gray powder wherever it steps.

attacks.name: Dust Slam
attacks.flavor: A heavy fist of packed gravel slams down, sending a shockwave of grit into your eyes.

attacks.name: Bone Shard Barrage
attacks.flavor: Cracks in its torso open briefly, spitting shards of ancient femur at close range.

specialAbilities: Unyielding Stance: Cannot be pushed or knocked back unless reduced to 0 HP. Immune to fear effects.
Choking Cloud: When hit by a blunt attack, releases a cloud of fine ash. All creatures within 10ft must make a CON save or suffer disadvantage on their next turn due to blindness.

tactics.specialBehavior: Plodding advance, using its massive bulk to block narrow passages. It prioritizes breaking shields or doors before striking living targets.

monster:ash-golem.firstAction: Stands immovable in the doorway, blocking escape, while slowly raising its arms to crush anyone who tries to rush past.
monster:ash-golem.lore: These constructs are the remnants of Aethelgard’s defensive automata, reanimated by the dragon’s lingering breath. They do not sleep; they simply stand guard over ruins that no longer exist, waiting for an enemy that already won.
monster:ash-golem.encounterText: The ground shudders as a mound of gray ash erupts into a towering silhouette. Dust cascades from its ribcage like rain as it turns its hollow head toward you, eyes burning with cold, white embers.

monster:whisper-wraith.name: Whisper Wraith
monster:whisper-wraith.description: A translucent, humanoid shape draped in tatters of spectral silk. Its face is a blur of motion, but its voice is perfectly clear—often sounding like someone you loved.

attacks.name: Soul Siphon
attacks.flavor: Cold fingers brush against your neck, pulling warmth out of your soul as easily as smoke.

attacks.name: Mimicry Strike
attacks.flavor: A spectral dagger made of solidified memory pierces your chest, freezing your blood.

specialAbilities: Phantom Voice: Can appear anywhere within line of sight where a sound has been made in the last round. Cannot be targeted while invisible.
Despair Aura: Enemies within 15ft must make a WIT save or gain one level of Exhaustion due to overwhelming sorrow.

tactics.specialBehavior: Use stealth and sound to isolate single targets. They prefer to strike from invisibility and retreat immediately after.

monster:whisper-wraith.firstAction: Vanishes into the shadows near the party’s healer, whispering a secret only they know to draw their attention away from the front line.
monster:whisper-wraith.lore: Trapped in the Spire of Echoes, these spirits mimic the voices of the dead to lure the living closer so they can feed on their grief. They remember being alive, but have forgotten why they died.
monster:whisper-wraith.encounterText: You hear your name called from behind a pillar. You turn, but see only a shimmering distortion in the air, vibrating with a sound that feels like a sob trapped in your throat.

monster:bone-wolf.name: Bone Wolf
monster:bone-wolf.description: A skeletal canine with ribs exposed and visible, pulsing with a faint green bioluminescence. Its jaw is unhinged, revealing rows of needle-like teeth.

attacks.name: Jaw Snap
attacks.flavor: Teeth clamp onto your limb with crushing force, shaking violently as it thrashes you aside.

attacks.name: Bone Rend
attacks.flavor: A blur of white claws slashes across your throat, leaving a trail of glowing green ichor.

specialAbilities: Pack Tactics: If two or more Bone Wolves are adjacent to the same target, all attacks against that target have advantage.
Glowing Trail: Leaves a faint phosphorescent trail for 1 hour. Can track any creature it has bitten.

tactics.specialBehavior: Flanking maneuvers. One wolf distracts while others flank. They target those with high armor first, trying to break defenses.

monster:bone-wolf.firstAction: Two wolves circle wide to the left and right, while the third crouches low, growling silently to fix your attention.
monster:bone-wolf.lore: Hunters of the White Forests, these wolves were once pets of the High Elves, now undead guardians of the borderlands. They hunt in silence, preferring to starve rather than eat carrion.
monster:bone-wolf.encounterText: In the pale moonlight, a pack of silhouettes detaches itself from the trees. They move with fluid grace, their bones clicking softly like dry twigs underfoot.

monster:ash-drake.name: Ash Drake
monster:ash-drake.description: A small, wingless dragon with scales like rough charcoal. It has no wings, but moves with surprising agility, dragging a long, spaded tail.

attacks.name: Charcoal Bite
attacks.flavor: Jaws clamp down, grinding teeth that taste of sulfur and regret.

attacks.name: Dust Cloud
attacks.flavor: A blast of superheated ash fills your lungs, burning your throat and blinding your eyes.

specialAbilities: Smoke Breath: Once per combat, exhales a 15ft cone of dense smoke. Creatures inside must succeed on a CON save or be blinded until the end of their next turn.
Scaly Hide: Reduces all incoming damage by 1 due to tough, ash-hardened skin.

tactics.specialBehavior: Uses terrain to ambush. Breathes dust to blind enemies, then bites. Prefers to fight alone to assert dominance.

monster:ash-drake.firstAction: Perches on a high beam, watching the party, then exhales a cone of thick gray smoke directly at the group’s center.
monster:ash-drake.lore: Miniature cousins of the Great Dragon, Ash Drakes breathe clouds of choking dust instead of fire. They are intelligent and cruel, often serving as scouts for larger draconic entities.
monster:ash-drake.encounterText: A hiss echoes from the rafters as a creature drops down, landing silently. It exhales a plume of gray smoke that smells of burnt hair and old stone.

monster:rust-knight.name: Rust Knight
monster:rust-knight.description: An undead soldier encased in corroded plate armor, rust bleeding from every joint. The helmet is a simple visor, hiding a face that may never have existed.

attacks.name: Rusty Slash
attacks.flavor: A jagged blade tears through flesh, leaving a wound that burns with metallic poison.

attacks.name: Shield Bash
attacks.flavor: A heavy iron slab slams into your chest, knocking the wind out of you.

specialAbilities: Oathbound: Cannot flee until all other enemies in the encounter are destroyed. Immune to charm effects.
Corrosive Touch: Any weapon that hits the Rust Knight takes 1 point of permanent corrosion (stacking) until repaired with magic or time.

tactics.specialBehavior: Slow but steady advance. Uses shield to block attacks, then strikes with heavy blows. Targets the most heavily armored opponent.

monster:rust-knight.firstAction: Steps forward, planting its feet firmly, and raises its shield to block the path, staring blankly ahead.
monster:rust-knight.lore: Remnants of the King’s Guard, these knights were buried in mass graves during the final siege. The earth rejected them, and they rose again, still holding their oath to defend a kingdom that is gone.
monster:rust-knight.encounterText: A clanking noise draws your eye. A knight rises from the mud, its armor flaking away like dead skin. It raises a sword that is more blade than hilt.

monster:mist-stalker.name: Mist Stalker
monster:mist-stalker.description: A shapeless entity of swirling fog, with two dark voids where eyes might be. It feels colder than the surrounding air.

attacks.name: Cold Touch
attacks.flavor: Fingers of ice wrap around your arm, stealing your warmth and leaving your skin numb.

attacks.name: Hope Drain
attacks.flavor: A wave of apathy washes over you, making the battle feel pointless and exhausting.

specialAbilities: Mist Form: Can move through spaces as small as 1 inch wide. Immune to non-magical damage.
Despair: On a hit, the target must make a WIT save or lose their next action due to sudden lethargy.

tactics.specialBehavior: Attacks from invisibility within the mist. Prioritizes draining the emotional resilience of leaders.

monster:mist-stalker.firstAction: Surrounds the party leader, whispering doubts into their ear, causing them to hesitate.
monster:mist-stalker.lore: Found in the valleys where the mist never lifts, these entities drain warmth and hope from those they touch. They are drawn to strong emotions, particularly fear and despair.
monster:mist-stalker.encounterText: The fog around you thickens suddenly, dropping the temperature by ten degrees. You feel a hand brush your cheek, though no one is there.

monster:crystal-spider.name: Crystal Spider
monster:crystal-spider.description: An arachnid with legs and body made of sharp, transparent glass. Light refracts through its form, making it nearly invisible in bright sunlight.

attacks.name: Shard Bite
attacks.flavor: Needle-like fangs pierce your armor, injecting a paralytic toxin.

attacks.name: Web Spray
attacks.flavor: A glob of sticky, glass-sharp webbing splatters across your face, blinding you.

specialAbilities: Refractive Camouflage: Has advantage on Dexterity checks when in direct sunlight. Cannot be targeted by ranged attacks if stationary and in sunlight.
Brittle Shell: Takes double damage from bludgeoning attacks, but half damage from slashing and piercing.

tactics.specialBehavior: Hangs from above, dropping onto prey. Uses webbing to immobilize targets, then delivers a killing bite.

monster:crystal-spider.firstAction: Descends from the ceiling on a silken thread, landing directly behind the party’s tank.
monster:crystal-spider.lore: Nestled in the crystal caves beneath Aethelgard, these spiders weave webs that cut through steel. They are prized by alchemists for their venom, which can dissolve metal.
monster:crystal-spider.encounterText: A glint of light catches your eye. Before you can focus, a spider descends on a thread of silver, its crystalline body catching the sun like a diamond.

monster:sorrow-giant.name: Sorrow Giant
monster:sorrow-giant.description: A towering figure of weeping stone, ten feet tall. Its face is a mask of eternal grief, and its tears burn like acid, pooling around its feet.

attacks.name: Stone Crush
attacks.flavor: A colossal foot stomps down, the impact shaking your bones.

attacks.name: Acidic Tear
attacks.flavor: A tear the size of a fist splashes onto your armor, eating through metal like acid.

specialAbilities: Mournful Roar: As a bonus action, lets out a cry of pure sorrow. All enemies within 30ft must make a WIT save or be frightened for 1 round.
Tear Pool: The ground around the giant becomes difficult terrain as pools of acid accumulate. Standing in a pool deals 1d6 acid damage at the start of each turn.

tactics.specialBehavior: Slow but powerful. Uses area-of-effect attacks to clear space. Will try to grab and crush opponents.

monster:sorrow-giant.firstAction: Raises a massive stone fist, bringing it down with enough force to crack the earth, sending shards flying outward.
monster:sorrow-giant.lore: Guardians of the Sunken Library, these giants were cursed to mourn the loss of all knowledge forever. Their tears are acidic, dissolving whatever they touch.
monster:sorrow-giant.encounterText: The ground shakes as a colossus rises from the earth. Stone cracks and shifts as it stands, and a sound like a thousand sighs fills the air. Tears stream down its face, hissing as they hit the ground.

monster:ash-hound.name: Ash Hound
monster:ash-hound.description: A dog-sized creature with fur made of gray ash and eyes like burning coals. It moves silently, leaving no tracks.

attacks.name: Ash Bite
attacks.flavor: Teeth sink into flesh, leaving a patch of ash that won’t wash off.

attacks.name: Smoke Spit
attacks.flavor: A jet of hot smoke blasts into your face, stinging your eyes.

specialAbilities: Fireproof: Takes half damage from fire sources.
Silent Step: Cannot be detected by hearing. Advantage on Stealth checks.

tactics.specialBehavior: Fast and agile. Attacks from stealth, targeting isolated individuals. Flee if injured significantly.

monster:ash-hound.firstAction: Leaps from the shadows, biting at the ankle of the nearest character.
monster:ash-hound.lore: Loyal to the dragon cultists, these hounds are fearless of fire and serve as scouts and hunters. They are often seen leading cultists to their victims.
monster:ash-hound.encounterText: You hear a low growl, and two pairs of ember eyes glow in the darkness. A hound leaps from the shadows, its mouth full of black smoke.

monster:ghost-archer.name: Ghost Archer
monster:ghost-archer.description: A spectral figure clad in the tattered armor of a long-dead ranger. It holds a bow made of bone, shooting arrows of cold light.

attacks.name: Cold Light Arrow
attacks.flavor: An arrow of frozen light pierces your chest, slowing your blood.

attacks.name: Spectral Shot
attacks.flavor: A ghostly shaft passes through your shield as if it weren’t there.

specialAbilities: Piercing Shot: Attacks ignore half cover and three-quarters cover. Armor class is reduced by 2 against these attacks.
Ethereal Movement: Can move through objects and creatures. Once per combat, can teleport up to 30ft.

tactics.specialBehavior: Stays at range, picking off key targets. Moves frequently to avoid counter-attacks.

monster:ghost-archer.firstAction: Draws an arrow of cold light and fires it at the party’s spellcaster.
monster:ghost-archer.lore: Spectral marksmen who haunt the borders of Aethelgard. Their arrows ignore physical armor, piercing straight to the heart.
monster:ghost-archer.encounterText: An arrow whizzes past your ear, embedding itself in the wall with a chill. You look up to see a ghostly archer standing on a ridge, aiming directly at you.

monster:root-horror.name: Root Horror
monster:root-horror.description: A massive tree that has come alive, with root-like limbs that drag prey into the earth. Its bark is scarred with faces of those it has consumed.

attacks.name: Root Grasp
attacks.flavor: Thick roots wrap around your legs, pulling you down into the dirt.

attacks.name: Wood Split
attacks.flavor: A massive branch swings down, cracking like a whip.

specialAbilities: Entangling Roots: As a bonus action, can create a zone of difficult terrain in a 20ft radius. Creatures starting their turn in the zone are restrained.
Life Drain: When a creature is grappled, the Root Horror heals 5 HP at the start of its turn.

tactics.specialBehavior: Grapples targets and drags them into its roots. Uses area attacks to control space.

monster:root-horror.firstAction: Slams its root-like limbs into the ground, creating entangling vines that restrict movement.
monster:root-horror.lore: Once ordinary trees, these horrors were twisted by the dragon’s influence. They drag prey into the earth, feeding on their life force to grow stronger.
monster:root-horror.encounterText: The forest floor ripples. A tree trunk splits open, revealing a maw of gnarled wood and thorns. Roots snake out, grabbing at your ankles.

monster:dream-eater.name: Dream Eater
monster:dream-eater.description: A small, bat-like creature with large, luminous eyes. It feeds on the memories of sleeping humans, leaving them empty and confused.

attacks.name: Memory Sip
attacks.flavor: A cold sensation in your mind, as if a favorite memory is being erased.

attacks.name: Bat Bite
attacks.flavor: Sharp teeth nip at your finger, drawing a drop of blood.

specialAbilities: Memory Theft: On a hit, the target loses a random memory (GM choice). If the target has no memories left, they become incapacitated.
Fragile: Takes double damage from any source.

tactics.specialBehavior: Attacks sleeping or unconscious targets. Flies away quickly if disturbed.

monster:dream-eater.firstAction: Hovers over a sleeping PC, preparing to feed on their dreams.
monster:dream-eater.lore: Found in the bedrooms of the wealthy, these creatures feed on memories. They are harmless individually, but a swarm can erase a person’s identity.
monster:dream-eater.encounterText: You wake to find a small, furry creature perched on your chest. It opens its mouth, and you feel a piece of your childhood slipping away.

encounter:the-banquet-of-shadows.name: The Banquet of Shadows
encounter:the-banquet-of-shadows.description: Lord Harrow hosts a final dinner in his crumbling manor, where the guests are spectral echoes of nobles long dead. They speak in riddles of past glories and demand tales from the living to sustain their fading forms.
encounter:the-banquet-of-shadows.text: The long table is set with silver that hasn’t tarnished in three centuries. At its head sits Lord Harrow, immaculate in velvet, though his eyes hold the hollow patience of a man hosting a funeral he cannot leave. Around him sit twelve empty chairs occupied by translucent figures that ripple like candle smoke. One turns its gaze toward you. "Sit," it whispers. "Tell us what the sky looked like before the ash fell."
encounter:the-banquet-of-shadows.gmGuidance: Treat this as a roleplay-heavy scene. Players can answer questions, offer memories, or lie. Success grants temporary boons (inspiration, minor healing, or clues about the dragon's schedule). Failure means the ghosts grow restless, draining vitality or demanding a physical token.
outcomes: Ghosts share a fragment of royal cipher
Ghosts demand a memory as payment
Lord Harrow reveals a hidden passage behind the tapestry

encounter:assault-on-the-bridge.name: Assault on the Bridge
encounter:assault-on-the-bridge.description: Bone wolves harry the party across the narrow span of the Bridge of Sighs, using the wind and crumbling stonework to pick off stragglers.
encounter:assault-on-the-bridge.text: The wind howls through the arches, carrying the scent of dry rot and old blood. From the mist below, pale shapes detach themselves from the parapets. Three bone wolves drop onto the stone, ribs rattling like castanets, jaws dripping with centuries of dust.
encounter:assault-on-the-bridge.gmGuidance: Focus on terrain hazards. The bridge narrows. Wolves have high AGI and prefer flanking. Use the environment for tactical advantages.
outcomes: Party secures the far end but loses supplies
Alpha wolf flees with a stolen banner

encounter:the-library-flood.name: The Library Flood
encounter:the-library-flood.description: Black water seeps into the Sunken Library of Vael, threatening to dissolve centuries of lore. The party must navigate rising currents and shifting shelves to retrieve a specific scroll.
encounter:the-library-flood.text: Water laps at the first tier of reading desks, thick and ink-black. Scrolls float like sleeping birds. Somewhere above, the muffled crack of splintering oak marks the collapse of the upper galleries. You have moments before the current drags everything deeper.
encounter:the-library-flood.gmGuidance: Time pressure is key. Use skill tests to manage movement, retrieval, and avoiding drowning or losing items. Emphasize the tragedy of losing knowledge.
outcomes: Current sweeps a shelf down, blocking exit

choices.text: Dive into the ink-black water to secure the scroll before the current takes it
choices.successOutcome: You grip the wax-sealed cylinder just as a pillar collapses nearby, securing vital historical records.
choices.failureOutcome: You surface gasping, clutching the scroll, but a submerged bookshelf pins your leg, costing precious time and forcing a companion to drag you free while water rises another inch.

choices.text: Climb the leaning shelving units to reach the higher alcove where the scroll rests
choices.successOutcome: You vault across the slick wood and snatch the scroll from a dry niche, establishing a stable footing for the team.
choices.failureOutcome: Your boot slips on damp parchment; you catch yourself but knock loose a cascade of brittle folios that sink instantly, obscuring the target alcove in drifting paper.

encounter:the-dragons-gaze.name: The Dragon’s Gaze
encounter:the-dragons-gaze.description: The party observes the colossal eye of the slumbering dragon from a high vantage point. Its pupils dilate and contract in patterns that predict where its next breath of decay will strike.
encounter:the-dragons-gaze.text: Through the rift in the cloud layer, the great eye dominates the horizon. It does not blink. Instead, the iris shifts—slowly, deliberately—tracking invisible points across the valley below. The air grows heavy, tasting of copper and old fire.
encounter:the-dragons-gaze.gmGuidance: Players must match pupil movements to landmarks on a map or terrain features. Correct prediction allows them to avoid the attack or position themselves advantageously. Wrong prediction results in environmental damage or forced repositioning.

choices.text: Trace the dilation rhythm against the topographical markers to forecast the impact zone
choices.successOutcome: The pattern resolves into a clear trajectory; you call out the safe corridor just as the valley floor cracks open.
choices.failureOutcome: You misjudge the lag between pupil shift and thermal buildup; the predicted safe zone erupts in geysers of superheated ash, forcing everyone to scramble backward and lose their footing.

choices.text: Use reflective surfaces to bounce sunlight into the dragon’s cornea, testing its reaction timing
choices.successOutcome: The flash triggers a micro-twitch; the pupil snaps shut precisely where you marked, confirming the strike coordinates.
choices.failureOutcome: The glare startles the beast slightly; its gaze jerks unpredictably, causing a secondary tremor that cracks the ledge beneath you and sends loose gravel tumbling into the abyss.

encounter:the-ash-storm.name: The Ash Storm
encounter:the-ash-storm.description: Visibility drops to zero as a sudden gale of fine gray powder swallows the landscape. Whispers rise from the storm itself, coaxing travelers to walk into cliffs or open graves.
encounter:the-ash-storm.text: The world shrinks to the length of your arm. Ash falls like snow, coating lungs and eyelids. Beneath the wind’s low moan, voices begin to speak your name—not from behind, but from within the gray. They sound familiar. They sound like home.
encounter:the-ash-storm.gmGuidance: Force players to rely on hearing, touch, or magical senses. Whispers tempt them to abandon formation or seek "rest". Tests determine if they resist or succumb temporarily.
outcomes: Party holds formation and finds shelter
One character follows a whisper into danger
Storm passes revealing a hidden ruin

choices.text: Bind yourselves together with rope and march by counting steps aloud to maintain cohesion
choices.successOutcome: The steady rhythm grounds you; the whispers fade to background static as you push through the whiteout.
choices.failureOutcome: A sudden gust knocks the lead walker off balance; the tension snaps the line, separating the group and scattering voices into isolated hallucinations that pull two members apart.

choices.text: Close your eyes and listen for the natural wind shear to find the leeward side of the ridge
choices.successOutcome: You hear the subtle change in pitch that marks the sheltered cleft, guiding the party safely into stone.
choices.failureOutcome: The ash muffles the wind’s true direction; you lead the party toward a false echo, walking straight into a shallow sinkhole that traps ankles and costs half your remaining daylight.

encounter:guarding-the-gate.name: Guarding the Gate
encounter:guarding-the-gate.description: Garrick stands alone before the iron-banded gate, refusing entry until visitors prove they remember why they fight. He demands a story, not a sword.
encounter:guarding-the-gate.text: Garrick leans against the cold iron, armor scarred but polished, a rusted blade resting across his knees. He doesn’t look up as you approach. "Everyone has steel these days," he mutters. "But steel doesn’t remember. What do you carry that won’t turn to dust?"
encounter:guarding-the-gate.gmGuidance: Roleplay test. Players can recite lineage, perform a feat of loyalty, or offer a genuine memory. Garrick respects authenticity over bravado.
outcomes: Garrick tests them with a second question
Refusal forces a detour through the marshes

encounter:the-forest-of-bones.name: The Forest of Bones
encounter:the-forest-of-bones.description: Claustrophobic woods where petrified trees interlock like cage bars. Hidden predators stalk from above and below, using the dense bone-littered floor for ambushes.
encounter:the-forest-of-bones.text: Sunlight fractures through a canopy of calcified branches. Every step crunches on ribcages and femurs buried in loam. From the shadows between trunks, something breathes—a wet, rhythmic sound that matches your own heartbeat too closely.
encounter:the-forest-of-bones.gmGuidance: Tight space limits movement. Enemies have cover bonuses. Use stealth and verticality. Root horrors and ash hounds work well here.
outcomes: Party trapped in a bone-circle, forced to fight defensively
Discover a safe grove with ancient markers

encounter:the-seers-vision.name: The Seer’s Vision
encounter:the-seers-vision.description: Elara draws the party into a shared dream-state where fragments of possible futures flicker like stained glass. They must piece together a coherent warning from disjointed imagery.
encounter:the-seers-vision.text: Elara’s blind eyes weep silver tears as she grips your hands. The ground dissolves. You stand in a courtyard of floating mirrors, each reflecting a different ending: a crown of ash, a silent bell, a hand letting go. The air hums with unspoken warnings.
encounter:the-seers-vision.gmGuidance: Players interpret symbols. Correct interpretation yields a crucial clue or buff. Misinterpretation leads to a temporary debuff or misdirection. Emphasize the elegiac tone.
outcomes: Fragments show a betrayal among allies

choices.text: Focus on the mirror showing the silent bell and trace its connection to the crown of ash
choices.successOutcome: The correlation clicks: the bell tolls when the dragon's hunger peaks, revealing a predictable window for action.
choices.failureOutcome: You force a link that isn't there; the vision distorts, flooding your mind with conflicting timelines that leave you disoriented and suffering a brief penalty to concentration.

choices.text: Touch the mirror depicting the hand letting go to absorb its emotional resonance directly
choices.successOutcome: The tactile feedback anchors the dream; you feel the weight of the coming sacrifice and gain clarity on who must stay behind.
choices.failureOutcome: The grief hits too hard; you stumble, dragging a companion down with you, and the vision fractures prematurely, leaving you with a haunting echo of loss that saps morale.

encounter:the-cultist-ritual.name: The Cultist Ritual
encounter:the-cultist-ritual.description: Vexis and his followers prepare to sacrifice a bound prisoner, believing the dragon’s arrival is divine salvation rather than consumption. The party must intervene without necessarily drawing blood.
encounter:the-cultist-ritual.text: Crimson robes sway in the heat haze. Vexis chants in a tongue that sounds like grinding stones, tracing sigils in the dust around a kneeling figure. The crowd watches with rapt, tear-streaked faces. "She comes to take us home," Vexis murmurs. "Do not delay the blessing."
encounter:the-cultist-ritual.gmGuidance: Social negotiation, persuasion, or intimidation. Players can appeal to the cultists' hope, expose logical flaws, or force a confrontation. Violence escalates quickly but might be necessary.
outcomes: Prisoner accepts fate, changing the ritual's outcome

encounter:the-spires-echo.name: The Spire’s Echo
encounter:the-spires-echo.description: Inside the Whispering Spire, accumulated voices reach a crescendo. The resonance shatters glass, cracks stone, and threatens to collapse the lower levels unless dampened.
encounter:the-spires-echo.text: The walls vibrate. Glass panes in the lantern brackets tremble, then pop with sharp cracks. The echo isn’t just sound anymore—it’s pressure. Your teeth ache. Dust sifts from the ceiling like premature snow.
encounter:the-spires-echo.gmGuidance: Players must choose actions to dampen the sound (muffling, redirecting, sacrificing objects) or endure the shaking. Tests focus on CON/WIT. Failure causes structural damage or noise attracts attention.

choices.text: Press heavy cloaks and pack blankets against the resonant stone pillars to absorb the frequency
choices.successOutcome: The fabric soaks up the vibration; the cracking slows, and a previously sealed door groans open as the pressure equalizes.
choices.failureOutcome: The material tears under the strain; a shard of crystallized sound bursts outward, slicing through a support beam and sending a cascade of rubble that narrows your escape route.

choices.text: Strike the central tuning stone with a metal instrument to create a counter-frequency
choices.successOutcome: The harmonic clash cancels the destructive resonance; the spire falls eerily silent, preserving the architecture for further exploration.
choices.failureOutcome: You hit the wrong interval; the dissonance amplifies temporarily, shattering the remaining windows and triggering a localized tremor that drops the temperature sharply and coats everyone in frost.

encounter:the-last-patrol.name: The Last Patrol
encounter:the-last-patrol.description: Commander Thorne’s skeletal remnant guards a forgotten road, moving with mechanical precision despite decades of decay. They don’t feel pain, only duty.
encounter:the-last-patrol.text: A phalanx of rusted plate marches in silence. Visors hide hollows where eyes once burned. Commander Thorne rides a bony destrier, his lance lowered, pointing directly at your chest. There is no war cry. Only the scrape of iron on stone.
encounter:the-last-patrol.gmGuidance: Emphasize their unyielding nature. They fight efficiently but lack adaptability. Terrain and clever tactics break their formation.
outcomes: Patrol overwhelmed but triggers alarm bells
Thorne recognizes a family sigil, pauses battle

encounter:the-hidden-archive.name: The Hidden Archive
encounter:the-hidden-archive.description: Behind a false wall in the Sunken Library, a secret chamber holds records of the dragon’s first pact. Pressure plates, acoustic locks, and memory-key mechanisms guard the entrance.
encounter:the-hidden-archive.text: The masonry feels wrong beneath your gloves—too uniform, too recent for a ruin this old. A hairline crack runs vertically through the plaster. When you press a specific sequence of stone carvings, a low harmonic hum answers from within.
encounter:the-hidden-archive.gmGuidance: Players must solve environmental puzzles using logic, observation, and possibly magical attunement. Failure delays discovery or damages artifacts. Success reveals vital lore.

choices.text: Follow the harmonic hum to locate the acoustic pressure plates and step on them in rhythm
choices.successOutcome: The stones depress in perfect sequence; the wall slides aside smoothly, revealing a climate-controlled vault untouched by the flood.
choices.failureOutcome: You miss the cadence by a fraction; a stone locks prematurely, triggering a spring-loaded mechanism that shoots a burst of ancient pollen into the room, coating everyone in sticky residue and blinding vision for a minute.

choices.text: Use a chisel to pry along the hairline fracture and listen for the hollow cavity behind the plaster
choices.successOutcome: You find the precise seam and apply pressure at the weak point; the panel gives way with minimal noise, granting quiet access.
choices.failureOutcome: Your tool slips and strikes a load-bearing keystone; a shower of dust and small stones rains down, collapsing part of the false wall and burying the entrance under rubble that must be cleared manually.

location:the-whispering-spire.name: The Whispering Spire
location:the-whispering-spire.description: The last standing tower of the capital, a jagged finger of grey stone piercing the smog-choked sky. Its walls are etched with runes that weep black sap when the wind howls from the north.
location:the-whispering-spire.atmosphere: A low, constant hum vibrates in the teeth. The air smells of ozone and old parchment. Shadows stretch longer than they should.
features: Climb the leaning belfry to reach the rookery where Throrion watches the horizon.
Bargain with the ghost-kings trapped in the walls for forgotten names of power.
Listen closely at the base of the tower to hear the specific tone that predicts an ash-storm.
Pick the rusted lock on the sealed chamber where the last map of the three continents is kept.
Hazard: The whispers induce madness (WIT check) if listened to for more than an hour without respite.

connections.travel: A steep descent through crumbling causeways where the ash drifts thickest. The climb takes an hour if you don't mind the vertigo.
connections.observation: You can see the skeletal remains of lower districts buried under white drifts below.
connections.risk: Wind gusts strong enough to knock a heavy armor-clad warrior off balance.

connections.travel: Descend into the bedrock via the Spire’s ancient service wells. The air grows cold and damp.
connections.observation: Damp moss clings to the brickwork, glowing faintly blue.
connections.risk: Falling debris or unstable stairs leading into darkness.

secrets: The Spire is not just a tower; it is a cage. The dragon sleeps beneath the capital, and the Spire’s weight keeps it down.

location:the-bone-white-forests.name: The Bone-White Forests
location:the-bone-white-forests.description: A vast woodland where the trees have turned to calcified bone, their branches like ribcages reaching for a sun they no longer see. The ground is soft with the dust of fallen leaves made of chalk.
location:the-bone-white-forests.atmosphere: Silence so profound it feels heavy. The crunch of footsteps sounds like breaking knuckles.
features: Scavenge the rib-cage trees for shards of bone-glass useful for crafting arrows.
Hide behind the massive femur-wall during the day to avoid the silent predators.
Trace the vein-maps etched into the largest tree to find the heart-root.
Barter with Vexis the Ash-Walker for safe passage through the predator hunting grounds.
Hazard: Silent predators stalk the shadows. Light attracts them.

connections.travel: Trudge through knee-deep drifts of fine white powder. The transition from forest to plain is gradual and disorienting.
connections.observation: The trees here lose their "ribs" and become simple pillars of white stone.
connections.risk: Getting lost in the identical landscape.

connections.travel: Navigate the labyrinthine root-systems that form natural tunnels deep underground.
connections.observation: Faint bioluminescent fungi cling to the ceiling bones.

secrets: The trees are not dead; they are waiting. If the ash covers them completely, they will wake.

location:the-ashen-marches.name: The Ashen Marches
location:the-ashen-marches.description: The borderlands covered in deep drifts of volcanic ash, stretching endlessly towards the horizon. Visibility is often less than ten paces. Magic is thin here, making spells fizzle or warp.
location:the-ashen-marches.atmosphere: A gritty taste in the mouth. The sky is a perpetual bruised purple-grey. Heat radiates from the ground.
features: Filter the ash through cloth to extract trace magical residue for alchemists.
Seek shelter in the hollowed-out carcass of a giant beast half-buried in the drifts.
Use the stillness to listen for the distant rumble of the dragon’s breathing.
Burn dried bone-wood for warmth, which produces a thick, choking smoke.
Hazard: Lung damage from prolonged exposure. Visibility drops to zero during storms.

connections.travel: A steep ascent up the crumbling causeway. The wind fights you every step.
connections.observation: The spire looms like a tombstone against the grey sky.
connections.risk: Wind gusts knocking travelers off the path.

connections.travel: Walk through the transitional zone where ash meets bone-dust. The terrain becomes uneven.
connections.observation: The silence of the forests begins to bleed into the wind.

connections.travel: Follow the dry riverbed south until it dips into the earth. The entrance is hidden under a landslide.
connections.observation: The air cools rapidly as you descend.

secrets: The ash is not just volcanic; it is the pulverized remains of the first dragonslayers.

location:the-sunken-library-of-vael.name: The Sunken Library of Vael
location:the-sunken-library-of-vael.description: An underground archive flooded with black water, where scrolls float like lily pads. The architecture is Elven, elegant but decayed, with statues of scholars holding their breath underwater.
location:the-sunken-library-of-vael.atmosphere: The smell of wet ink and rot. A eerie quiet broken only by dripping water. The water is perfectly still, reflecting nothing.
features: Dive into the black water to retrieve scrolls from the bottomless shelves.
Read the floating texts aloud to temporarily summon the ghosts of the librarians.
Break the seal on the Dry Section to access non-waterlogged maps.
Hide in the statue niches while avoiding the blind seer’s scrying.
Hazard: Drowning risk. The water induces hallucinations of past memories.

connections.travel: Ascend via the ancient service wells. The climb is slick with algae.
connections.observation: The sound of the wind returns as you near the surface.

connections.travel: Exit through the dry riverbed tunnel. The air outside is hot and dry.
connections.observation: The contrast between the cool dark and the hot ash is jarring.

secrets: The library contains the true name of the dragon, written in a language that eats memory.

location:the-kings-maw.name: The King’s Maw
location:the-kings-maw.description: A massive natural cavern system beneath the capital, shaped like a gaping jaw. It serves as the kingdom’s primary waste disposal and secret prison, where the rich and poor alike disappear.
location:the-kings-maw.atmosphere: Stench of decay and sulfur. Echoes amplify every footstep. Distant chains rattle.
features: Search the refuse piles for usable items dropped by the executed.
Negotiate with the prisoners in the deeper cells for information.
Follow the scent of sulfur to find the dragon’s warm spot.
Hide in the shadow of the massive jawbone structure.
Hazard: Gas pockets can ignite. Prisoners are desperate and violent.

connections.travel: Enter through the root-tunnels from above. The transition is seamless.
connections.observation: The roots hang down like stalactites, dripping fluid.

connections.travel: Climb the narrow spiral stair carved into the rock face. It leads directly to the bridge’s foundation.
connections.observation: You can see the executions happening above through cracks in the stone.

secrets: The King’s Maw connects directly to the dragon’s lair. The “waste” is actually offerings.

location:the-bridge-of-sighs.name: The Bridge of Sighs
location:the-bridge-of-sighs.description: A narrow stone bridge spanning a chasm, used for executions and final goodbyes. It is lined with the gravestones of those who died waiting for release.
location:the-bridge-of-sighs.atmosphere: A mournful wind whistles through the arches. The sound of sobbing is constant.
features: Listen to the sighs of the dead for clues about recent events.
Check the gravestones for names of missing persons.
Wait for the execution ceremony to observe the court’s hierarchy.
Jump from the bridge to escape pursuers (high risk).
Hazard: High winds can push characters off the edge. Emotional distress from the sounds.

connections.travel: Descend the spiral stair to the dungeon level.
connections.observation: The view of the chasm is dizzying.

connections.travel: Cross the bridge to enter the capital district leading to the spire.

secrets: The bridge was built over a rift where the dragon’s influence leaks into the world.

npc:thalorion-the-last.name: Thalorion the Last
npc:thalorion-the-last.description: A tall, gaunt figure with skin like parchment stretched over sharp bone. His eyes are milky white, blind to the present but seeing the flow of magic, and his hair is a cascade of silver dust that never falls to the ground.
npc:thalorion-the-last.personality: He speaks in a voice that sounds like dry leaves skittering on stone. He is polite, distant, and carries the weight of centuries without complaint. He moves with a predator’s grace but often pauses, as if listening to something no one else can hear.

motivation.want: To keep the Dragon’s gaze fixed solely on him until the last elf fades.
motivation.fear: That someone will break the ritual and let the beast turn its hunger toward the common folk.
motivation.lie: He believes his suffering is the only thing keeping the realm alive; he does not realize the realm is already ash, and he is merely delaying the inevitable.

dialogueHints: "The wind tastes of iron today. Do you hear it? The sky is hungry."
"I do not remember my mother’s face, but I remember the exact moment the first dragon egg cracked. That memory is heavy enough to sink a ship."

relationships.npc:mira-the-memory-eater: Mira holds the history Thalorion has forgotten; she is the living archive of the age he is losing.
relationships.npc:vexis-the-ash-walker: Vexis worships the force Thalorion is bargaining with; Thalorion views Vexis as a necessary madness.
relationships.npc:ser-kaelen-of-the-rusty-blade: Ser Kaelen guards the spire’s base; Thalorion relies on Kaelen’s stubbornness to hold the physical line while he fights the metaphysical one.

secrets: Thalorion is not just feeding the dragon; he is slowly becoming part of it. His shadow has begun to move independently.

npc:ser-kaelen-of-the-rusty-blade.name: Ser Kaelen of the Rusty Blade
npc:ser-kaelen-of-the-rusty-blade.description: A hulking man in plate armor that is pitted with red rust, despite being oiled daily. A jagged scar runs from his jaw to his collarbone, pulsing faintly when he draws his sword.
npc:ser-kaelen-of-the-rusty-blade.personality: Gritty, pragmatic, and deeply tired. He doesn’t care for court politics or high elven mysticism. He cares about holding the line. He mutters prayers to dead gods under his breath.

motivation.want: To die honorably defending the Spire, fulfilling his oath to the last king.
motivation.fear: Dying unnoticed, his sacrifice swallowed by the silence of the dying world.
motivation.lie: He thinks the curse on his blade is a punishment; it is actually a blessing that keeps him anchored to the mortal plane longer than any other knight.

dialogueHints: "The metal doesn't cut flesh anymore. It cuts time. Every swing takes a year off your life."
"Lord Harrow eats roast pheasant while I chew on leather jerky. Let him have his illusion of plenty."

relationships.npc:commander-thorne: Kaelen despises Thorne’s use of skeletons; he sees it as an insult to the dead who should rest.
relationships.npc:lord-harrow-of-the-empty-seat: Kaelen watches Harrow dine alone and wonders if the Lord is mad or just lonely.

secrets: The rust on his armor is actually dried blood from previous owners of the blade, who died trying to wield it.

npc:mira-the-memory-eater.name: Mira the Memory-Eater
npc:mira-the-memory-eater.description: A young woman with ink-stained fingers and eyes that seem to look through people. She wears robes made of stitched-together maps, each patch representing a lost province.
npc:mira-the-memory-eater.personality: Eccentric and distracted. She often forgets her own name but can recite the lineage of kings from three millennia ago. She touches objects to absorb their history, leaving them cold to others.

motivation.want: To record every last detail of Aethelgard before it vanishes completely.
motivation.fear: Forgetting herself entirely, becoming just another book in the library.
motivation.lie: She believes knowledge preserves things; in truth, her hoarding of memories is accelerating the decay of the physical world around her.

dialogueHints: "I don't know who I am, but I know how the First King broke his vow. It sounded like glass shattering."
"Ask me about the Battle of Red Sands. I was there. Well, my mind was. My body was somewhere else."

relationships.npc:lyra-the-song-weaver: Lyra’s songs trigger memories in Mira that are too painful to hold, causing Mira physical pain.
relationships.npc:elara-the-blind-seer: Mira protects Elara because Elara sees the end of the world that Mira is desperately trying to write down.

secrets: Mira is not just remembering the past; she is rewriting it slightly with each retelling, subtly altering history to make it more bearable.

npc:vexis-the-ash-walker.name: Vexis the Ash-Walker
npc:vexis-the-ash-walker.description: A slender figure wrapped in layers of gray silk and ash-colored wool. Their face is obscured by a veil woven from spider-silk, and they leave footprints that fill with ash immediately after stepping.
npc:vexis-the-ash-walker.personality: Ethereal and calm, speaking in soft riddles. They find beauty in destruction and view the kingdom’s decline as a purification process.

motivation.want: To accelerate the dragon’s awakening so the final transformation can begin.
motivation.fear: Being left behind in a world that refuses to die quietly.
motivation.lie: They believe the Dragon is a god of mercy; in reality, the Dragon is a mindless hunger that will consume everything, including the cultists.

dialogueHints: "Why cling to the crumbling wall when the horizon promises such exquisite silence?"
"The ash is not death. It is the world exhaling."

relationships.npc:brother-silas: Vexis and Silas share a dogma, but Vexis finds Silas too rigid in his rituals.
relationships.npc:thalorion-the-last: Vexis sees Thalorion’s resistance as a form of vanity; he should surrender to the sky.

secrets: Vexis has already eaten a piece of the Dragon’s scale, granting them minor immortality but making them colder than the grave.

npc:lord-harrow-of-the-empty-seat.name: Lord Harrow of the Empty Seat
npc:lord-harrow-of-the-empty-seat.description: A portly man in velvet robes that are fraying at the hem. He has a nervous tic where he constantly checks a pocket watch that has stopped ticking.
npc:lord-harrow-of-the-empty-seat.personality: Delusional but charming. He insists on elaborate dinner parties where he converses with empty chairs. He is terrified of being alone but hides it behind performative grandeur.

motivation.want: To maintain the appearance of the Sovereignty’s glory until the very last moment.
motivation.fear: The silence of the empty hall.
motivation.lie: He believes his guests are merely delayed; he cannot accept that everyone he loves is either dead or forgotten.

dialogueHints: "Lord Pendergast is late again. Typical. Tell the cook to keep the pheasant warm."
"Do you hear that? The music hasn't stopped yet. It’s playing for us."

relationships.npc:ser-kaelen-of-the-rusty-blade: Kaelen stands guard outside Harrow’s dining hall, enduring the Lord’s rambling monologues.
relationships.npc:garrick-the-gatekeeper: Harrow pays Garrick double to pretend visitors are real guests, fueling his delusion.

secrets: The "empty seats" are filled with the ghosts of Harrow’s family, which only he can see. He talks to them because they are the only ones who understand.

npc:elara-the-blind-seer.name: Elara the Blind Seer
npc:elara-the-blind-seer.description: A small child with large, clouded gray eyes. She wears a simple tunic and always carries a string of black beads, which she counts obsessively.
npc:elara-the-blind-seer.personality: Quiet and solemn. She speaks in short, blunt statements about doom. She does not cry or laugh, observing the world with ancient weariness.

motivation.want: To warn people of the end, even if they don’t listen.
motivation.lie: She believes she can change the future if she speaks loudly enough; she cannot, she can only predict it.

dialogueHints: "The sky is gray. The ground is gray. Soon, we will be gray."
"You will die on the bridge. Not today. But soon."

relationships.npc:mira-the-memory-eater: Mira listens to Elara’s predictions to add them to her archives, preserving the prophecy of the end.
relationships.npc:lyra-the-song-weaver: Lyra sings lullabies to Elara to help her sleep, trading memories of her own childhood for moments of peace for the girl.

secrets: Elara is not human. She is a vessel for the Dragon’s consciousness, waiting for the right moment to fully awaken.

npc:garrick-the-gatekeeper.name: Garrick the Gatekeeper
npc:garrick-the-gatekeeper.description: An old man with a back bent like a bow. He sits on a wooden stool in front of the massive iron gates of the capital, holding a spear that is older than the city.
npc:garrick-the-gatekeeper.personality: Stubborn and literal. He follows the rules exactly as written, regardless of context. He hates change and new faces.

motivation.want: To keep the gate closed and unchanged for another day.
motivation.fear: The gate opening, letting something in (or out) that cannot be undone.
motivation.lie: He thinks he is protecting the city from invaders; he is actually preventing refugees from fleeing a dying land.

dialogueHints: "No entry. No exit. Unless you have the King’s signet. Or unless you’re already dead."
"Fifty years I’ve sat here. The wood of this stool is part of my bones now."

relationships.npc:lord-harrow-of-the-empty-seat: Harrow bribes Garrick to ignore certain "guests," creating a secret passage for the nobility.
relationships.npc:commander-thorne: Thorne tries to conscript Garrick’s few remaining men, but Garrick refuses to release anyone from the gate.

secrets: Garrick is immortal, cursed by the same magic that binds Thalorion. He cannot leave the gate because he *is* the gate.

npc:lyra-the-song-weaver.name: Lyra the Song-Weaver
npc:lyra-the-song-weaver.description: A woman with a hauntingly beautiful voice and hands scarred from plucking strings. Her eyes are hollow, reflecting the memories she has given away.
npc:lyra-the-song-weaver.personality: Melancholic and gentle. She sings to soothe pain but loses pieces of herself with every song. She is always looking for a melody that feels familiar.

motivation.want: To heal the wounds of the kingdom, even if it costs her identity.
motivation.fear: Forgetting the faces of those she loved most.
motivation.lie: She believes her sacrifices are noble; they are also selfish, as she clings to the role of healer to avoid facing her own emptiness.

dialogueHints: "This song... I sang it to my daughter once. Did I have a daughter? The melody says yes."
"Let me sing. It won’t hurt. Much."

relationships.npc:mira-the-memory-eater: Lyra gives her songs to Mira, who stores them as memories, creating a strange symbiosis.
relationships.npc:elara-the-blind-seer: Lyra sings to Elara to ease her visions, trading her own past for the girl’s comfort.

secrets: Lyra has forgotten her own name. She answers to "Song-Weaver" because she cannot recall what she was called before.

npc:brother-silas.name: Brother Silas
npc:brother-silas.description: A tall man wearing a mask carved from a single piece of black dragon scale. His voice is muffled and resonant, echoing as if from a deep well.
npc:brother-silas.personality: Zealous and serene. He preaches acceptance of the ash and the dragon’s fire. He is not cruel, but he is indifferent to individual suffering.

motivation.want: To convert the populace to the faith of the Dragon, ensuring their souls are consumed rather than lost.
motivation.lie: He believes the Dragon is benevolent; he fears that if he admits it is hungry, his faith will crumble.

dialogueHints: "Breathe in. Taste the end? It tastes like salvation."
"Your tears are water. Your blood is ash. Why fight the transformation?"

relationships.npc:vexis-the-ash-walker: Silas and Vexis work together to spread the gospel of the Dragon, though they disagree on methods.
relationships.npc:ser-kaelen-of-the-rusty-blade: Kaelen spat on Silas’s sermon; Silas forgave him, which annoyed Kaelen more than anger would have.

secrets: The mask is fused to Silas’s face. If he removes it, he will suffocate, as he no longer breathes air, but ash.

npc:commander-thorne.name: Commander Thorne
npc:commander-thorne.description: A stern woman in battered steel armor, missing an eye covered by a patch. She carries a clipboard and constantly checks lists of names.
npc:commander-thorne.personality: Efficient and ruthless. She treats the war like a logistics problem. She doesn’t care if soldiers are alive, only if they are present.

motivation.want: To keep the army at full strength, regardless of the cost in lives or souls.
motivation.fear: Being told the war is over and there is nothing left to command.
motivation.lie: She believes order can save them; in reality, her rigid adherence to procedure is strangling the last hope of flexibility.

dialogueHints: "Casualties are acceptable. Absences are not. Report for duty."
"I don’t care if he’s a skeleton. He holds a pike. He is a soldier."

relationships.npc:ser-kaelen-of-the-rusty-blade: Thorne promotes Kaelen to keep him close, knowing his curse makes him a valuable asset in holding the line.
relationships.npc:garrick-the-gatekeeper: Thorne tries to draft Garrick’s "men" (who are mostly statues and stones), leading to bureaucratic friction.

secrets: Thorne knows the army is entirely composed of undead and constructs. She is the only one who remembers the last living soldier, and she keeps his name on her list.

faction:the-order-of-the-pale-flame.name: The Order of the Pale Flame
faction:the-order-of-the-pale-flame.description: A dwindling cadre of arcanists and archivists who tend the last literal and metaphorical fires of Aethelgard. They believe that as long as the ceremonial embers burn and the chronicles are read aloud, the realm retains enough substance to delay the dragon’s final consumption. To outsiders, they appear as melancholic hoarders; to themselves, they are the keepers of the kingdom’s soul.
goals: Sustain the Ember Liturgy by feeding it curated memories instead of traditional fuel
Preserve the High Elf king’s lineage records to anchor his fading immortality
Slow the kingdom’s physical decay by rationing remaining arcane ley-currents
resources: Control of the Sunken Library’s preserved grimoires and memory-crystals
Diplomatic patronage from the remnants of the noble houses
A network of ash-tenders who travel between manors collecting recollections

faction:the-cult-of-the-final-breath.reason: The Cult actively sabotages the Ember Liturgy and preaches that memory is a chain; the Order views them as grave-robbers of civilization

faction:the-oathsworn-remnant.reason: The Oathsworn guard the Order’s supply caravans and respect their duty-bound stewardship, though they resent the mages’ secrecy

hooks: A senior archivist begins forgetting their own name to keep the central brazier lit; players must choose whether to replace the fuel or let the scholar fade

Lyra discovers a ledger showing the Court has been secretly selling ancestral memories to foreign traders to buy time; the Order must decide whether to expose the theft or preserve the illusion of unity
secrets: The flame isn’t just holding the dragon at bay—it’s actively digesting the kingdom’s past. When it finally goes out, the dragon will leave, but every living soul will instantly lose all recorded history, language, and cultural context, leaving them feral and alone

faction:the-cult-of-the-final-breath.name: The Cult of the Final Breath
faction:the-cult-of-the-final-breath.description: Devotees who view the dragon’s eternal hunger not as destruction, but as a merciful exhalation. They dress in ash-grey veils, speak in hushed cadences, and treat death as a sacred homecoming. Where others see decay, they see a garden being cleared for winter. Their quiet fervor makes them more dangerous than outright rebels.
goals: Accelerate the realm’s gentle collapse by encouraging voluntary surrender to the dragon’s shadow
Collect “final breaths” from dying aristocrats to weave into the Dragon’s Favor tapestries
Infiltrate the royal funeral rites to ensure the sovereign’s last exhale feeds the beast directly
resources: Mastery of mortuary alchemy and ash-binding rituals
Influence over the grieving upper crust, who crave relief from centuries of martial fatigue
Hidden catacombs beneath the capital where the devout practice silent vigil

faction:the-order-of-the-pale-flame.reason: The Cult sees the Order’s memory-hoarding as selfish preservation; they deliberately sever ley-lines to starve the pale flame

faction:the-oathsworn-remnant.reason: The Oathsworn view the Cult as traitorous pacifists undermining the king’s war effort, though the Cult avoids open conflict unless protecting a ritual site

hooks: Nobles who die within the Cult’s sphere rise as calm, hollow echoes that repeat their last words; players are hired to determine if this is mercy or a slow rot spreading through the city
Vexis begins offering “peaceful exits” to wounded veterans; those who accept vanish into the Maw and return months later with scales under their skin, speaking in overlapping voices
secrets: Vexis has already been marked by the dragon’s breath. He doesn’t want the beast to consume the realm—he intends to shed his humanity and become the dragon’s new voice, using Lord Harrow as a temporary puppet monarch to legitimize the transition

faction:the-oathsworn-remnant.name: The Oathsworn Remnant
faction:the-oathsworn-remnant.description: The last standing host of Aethelgard’s former legions. Clad in tarnished plate and carrying banners that have long since lost their dye, they hold the eastern marches with grim discipline. They do not fight for glory or survival—they fight because the king swore they would, and in a dying world, keeping one’s word is the only magic left.
goals: Maintain the defensive perimeter at the Ashen Marches until the promised reinforcements arrive
Preserve military logistics and supply depots to prevent total societal collapse
Find a way to break or fulfill the sovereign’s ancient vow without shattering the army’s morale
resources: Veteran heavy infantry and siege engineers who remember when the marches were fertile
Fortified watchtowers and controlled river crossings
Direct courier raven lines to the High Elf king’s mobile court

faction:the-order-of-the-pale-flame.reason: The Remnant relies on the Order’s scrying lenses to coordinate defenses, but suspects the mages are prioritizing archival preservation over tactical intelligence

faction:the-cult-of-the-final-breath.reason: The Cult’s quiet defections and sabotage of supply caches are seen as treason; the Oathsworn execute captured cultists without ceremony to set an example

hooks: Commander Thorne seals the garrison gates not from enemy pressure, but because a “memory-leak” plague is turning soldiers into weeping stone statues; he needs outsiders to retrieve the source artifact from the Whispering Spire
Ser Kaelen discovers that the enemy vanguard marching against them wears identical sigils to Aethelgard’s own lost regiments—just older, bleached by ash and time
secrets: The “reinforcements” the king promised arrived decades ago: the dragon itself, cloaked in a storm of ash-wraiths that mimic legion formations. The Oathsworn have been fighting their own allies in the perpetual fog, too exhausted and honor-bound to realize the war ended generations ago

situation:the-kings-fade.name: The King’s Fade
situation:the-kings-fade.description: Thalorion, the High Elf and anchor of the realm’s reality, is physically dissolving into ash. The court claims it is a mere illness, but those who look closely see his edges blurring into the air. If he fades completely, the Dragon will wake from its slumber to consume the world.

risks: Thalorion, the High Elf and anchor of the realm’s reality, is physically dissolving into ash. The court claims it is a mere illness, but those who look closely see his edges blurring into the air. If he fades completely, the Dragon will wake from its slumber to consume the world.

actors.agenda: To maintain the illusion of solidity until the next eclipse, buying time for the ritual to be found.
actors.leverage: His physical presence is the only thing keeping the Dragon dormant; his panic would break the spell instantly.

defaultAction.now: Sits motionless on the Throne of Embers, forcing himself to breathe slowly to keep his chest rising and falling visibly.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Begins to miss meals entirely, leaving plates full as he forgets he has a mouth, causing rumors of madness to spread.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: His left hand becomes translucent during a council meeting; he hides it under the table while continuing to speak, risking a total collapse if he gestures too wildly.

actors.agenda: To consume Thalorion’s memories before he fades, preserving them in her own mind to prevent the kingdom from forgetting itself.
actors.leverage: She is the only one who can sense the rate of his fading without touching him.

defaultAction.now: Hovers in the shadows of the Spire’s antechamber, watching Thalorion with hungry, sad eyes.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Approaches a high-ranking noble and eats their memory of the King’s health, making them believe he is stronger than he is.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Attempts to eat the memory of the Dragon’s hunger from Thalorion himself, which could kill him or save him depending on success.

clock.name: The Dissolution
clock.doom: Thalorion vanishes completely; the sky turns black and the Dragon wakes.
clock.pauseCondition: Players discover the source of the fading (a curse, a sacrifice, or natural end) and act to stabilize or accept it.

stages.name: Flickering
stages.description: Thalorion’s voice echoes slightly when he speaks alone.

consequences.narrative: Nobles begin to whisper that the King is already dead.

stages.name: Translucence
stages.description: Objects behind Thalorion are visible through his torso.

trigger.eventDescription: He fails to perform a daily ritual or is struck by doubt.

consequences.narrative: His shadow detaches and moves independently.

stages.name: Dispersal
stages.description: Parts of his body drift away like smoke when he moves.

consequences.narrative: The temperature in the Spire drops to freezing.

stages.name: Vanishing
stages.description: Thalorion is gone; only his crown remains floating.

trigger.eventDescription: The Dragon roars in its sleep.

consequences.narrative: Gravity begins to fail in the capital city.

outgoingLeads.information: Thalorion is not dying naturally; he is being eaten by something external.

discovery.description: Elara reveals that she sees "teeth" in the King’s aura when she touches his pulse.

outgoingLeads.information: Mira is feeding on the same essence that is draining Thalorion.

discovery.description: Players notice Mira grows more solid and vibrant exactly when Thalorion becomes more translucent.

outgoingLeads.information: The Dragon’s dreams are leaking into reality, causing the fade.
