discovery.description: An ancient text describes "The Symbiosis of Ash and Dream," linking the King’s life force to the Dragon’s subconscious.

victory.description: The The King’s Fade is resolved successfully

failure.description: The The King’s Fade ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: Thalorion, the High Elf and anchor of the realm’s reality, is physically dissolving into ash. The court claims it is a mere illness, but those who look closely see his edges blurring into the air. If he fades completely, the Dragon will wake from its slumber to consume the world.

situation:the-ash-winter.name: The Ash Winter
situation:the-ash-winter.description: The ash drifts are rising, threatening to bury the last arable land. The wind carries not just dust, but fragments of memory, causing those who breathe it deeply to lose themselves.

risks: The ash drifts are rising, threatening to bury the last arable land. The wind carries not just dust, but fragments of memory, causing those who breathe it deeply to lose themselves.

actors.agenda: To harvest the ash for alchemical use, believing it contains the soul-stuff of the dead.
actors.leverage: She controls the only functional air-filtering masks made from dragon-scale.

defaultAction.now: Directs laborers to dig trenches around the city walls to divert the ash flow.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Bribes the gatekeepers to let her export the purest ash, leaving the poor with less filtered air.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Uses the concentrated ash to create a temporary shield, but it requires sacrificing the memories of the workers who handle it.

actors.agenda: To preach that the ash is God’s penance, encouraging acceptance rather than resistance.
actors.leverage: Influence over the common folk who are tired of fighting a losing battle.

defaultAction.now: Prefects in the market square, telling people to inhale the ash to purify their souls.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Organizes a procession into the deep ash drifts, where several participants do not return.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Claims that the Lost Heir is coming to cleanse the ash, using the rumor to consolidate power.

clock.name: The Burying
clock.doom: The capital is buried under thirty feet of ash; the remaining survivors are trapped underground, breathing recycled air.
clock.pauseCondition: Players clear the primary ash vents or secure the food supply.

stages.name: Drifting
stages.description: Ash covers the streets; visibility is reduced.

consequences.narrative: Crops in the outer fields die overnight.

stages.name: Choking
stages.description: Ash enters buildings through cracks; lungs fill up.

trigger.eventDescription: A strong wind shifts direction toward the city.

consequences.narrative: Children stop playing outside; coughing becomes constant.

stages.name: Buried
stages.description: The ground floor of every building is sealed under ash.

consequences.narrative: Food stores in cellars are contaminated by ash.

stages.name: Entombed
stages.description: The city is an island in a sea of gray.

trigger.eventDescription: The main aquifer clogs with silt.

consequences.narrative: Desperation leads to riots over clean water.

outgoingLeads.information: The ash contains the memories of the dead, making it valuable for lorekeepers.

discovery.description: Vexis sells a vial of "pure ash" that shows visions when held to the eye.

outgoingLeads.information: Brother Silas believes the ash will reveal the location of the Lost Heir.

discovery.description: Silas preaches that the Heir will rise from the ash, leading pilgrims to the worst drifts.

outgoingLeads.information: The ash originates from a specific crack in the earth near the Bone White Forests.

discovery.description: Tracking the ash flow leads players to a magical fissure where the Oath was broken.

victory.description: The The Ash Winter is resolved successfully

failure.description: The The Ash Winter ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: The ash drifts are rising, threatening to bury the last arable land. The wind carries not just dust, but fragments of memory, causing those who breathe it deeply to lose themselves.

situation:the-courts-denial.name: The Court’s Denial
situation:the-courts-denial.description: The nobles are throwing lavish parties while the walls crumble, ignoring the truth. They believe that if they act grand enough, the Dragon will respect them and spare them.

risks: The nobles are throwing lavish parties while the walls crumble, ignoring the truth. They believe that if they act grand enough, the Dragon will respect them and spare them.

actors.agenda: To marry his daughter to the Dragon (symbolically) to secure a place in the new order.
actors.leverage: Wealth and control of the grain supply.

defaultAction.now: Hosts a banquet where the food is served on gold plates, but the wine is watered down to save costs.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Secretly negotiates with the Dragon-Cultists to betray the King if the Dragon wins.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Locks the granaries and demands tribute from the commoners to fund the wedding feast.

actors.agenda: To compose a song so beautiful that the Dragon will pause to listen, buying time.
actors.leverage: The cultural influence of her music; she can sway public opinion.

defaultAction.now: Performs nightly in the Spire, weaving memories of better times into the melody.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Her song causes listeners to forget their fears, making them complacent about the approaching doom.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: She realizes her song is actually lulling the Dragon deeper into sleep, but at the cost of her own memory.

clock.name: The Hollow Crown
clock.doom: The nobles are slaughtered by the Dragon while feasting, proving their insignificance.
clock.pauseCondition: Players expose Harrow’s treachery or prove Lyra’s song is futile.

stages.name: Gilded Cage
stages.description: The parties are opulent, but the guests look pale and tired.

consequences.narrative: Rumors of the King’s fading spread despite the festivities.

stages.name: Fracture
stages.description: Nobles begin to turn on each other for resources.

trigger.eventDescription: A wall segment collapses during a ball.

consequences.narrative: Lords blame the guards instead of acknowledging structural decay.

stages.name: Flight
stages.description: Key nobles prepare secret escape routes.

consequences.narrative: The city gates are guarded by loyalists, not the nobles’ men.

stages.name: Massacre
stages.description: The Dragon breathes fire into the Spire.

consequences.narrative: The nobles burn alive in their silks.

outgoingLeads.information: Lord Harrow is hoarding grain and negotiating with the cultists.

discovery.description: Hidden ledgers in Harrow’s estate show payments to Dragon-Cultists.

outgoingLeads.information: Lyra’s song is weakening the magical wards because she is giving her own life force to it.

discovery.description: Players notice Lyra ages visibly after each performance.

outgoingLeads.information: The Dragon respects strength and grandeur, which is why the court’s denial is partially effective.

discovery.description: Thorne notes that the Dragon pauses when the Spire lights are brightest.

victory.description: The The Court’s Denial is resolved successfully

failure.description: The The Court’s Denial ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: The nobles are throwing lavish parties while the walls crumble, ignoring the truth. They believe that if they act grand enough, the Dragon will respect them and spare them.

situation:the-dragons-dream.name: The Dragon’s Dream
situation:the-dragons-dream.description: The dragon is dreaming, and its dreams are manifesting as monsters in the real world. These creatures are twisted reflections of the kingdom’s history and fears.

risks: The dragon is dreaming, and its dreams are manifesting as monsters in the real world. These creatures are twisted reflections of the kingdom’s history and fears.

actors.agenda: To hunt the dream-monsters, believing that killing them will calm the Dragon.
actors.leverage: Experience and a sword forged from dragon bone.

defaultAction.now: Patrols the edge of the Bone White Forests, killing minor dream-beasts.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Chases a large dream-beast into the city, causing collateral damage.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Realizes the beasts are manifestations of his own past failures and begins to fear entering the forest.

actors.agenda: To keep the dream-monsters out of the city at all costs, even if it means sealing off entire districts.
actors.leverage: Control of the city gates and the guard force.

defaultAction.now: Seals the western district, trapping civilians inside to contain a breach.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Executes a soldier who opened the gate too late, claiming it let a beast in.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Burns the western district to destroy any remaining beasts, killing hundreds.

clock.name: The Bleeding World
clock.doom: Reality fractures; the city exists in two places at once — the present and the Dragon’s nightmare.
clock.pauseCondition: Players enter the Dragon’s dream and stabilize it, or kill the Dream-Eater.

stages.name: Whispers
stages.description: Dream-creatures appear briefly, then vanish.

consequences.narrative: People report seeing ghosts of their ancestors.

stages.name: Manifestation
stages.description: Dream-creatures stay for hours and can be killed.

trigger.eventDescription: A major historical tragedy is commemorated.

consequences.narrative: The geometry of the city begins to shift; streets loop back on themselves.

stages.name: Overlap
stages.description: Dream and reality occupy the same space.

consequences.narrative: Time flows differently in different districts.

stages.name: Nightmare
stages.description: The Dragon opens one eye.

consequences.narrative: All magic in the city goes wild.

outgoingLeads.information: The dream-monsters are formed from the memories of the dead.

discovery.description: Killing a wraith releases a burst of memories that players can read.

outgoingLeads.information: The Bone White Forests are the physical anchor of the Dragon’s dream.

discovery.description: Trees in the forest are made of stone and bleed ash instead of sap.

outgoingLeads.information: Kaelen’s rusted blade is the only weapon that can permanently kill dream-creatures.

discovery.description: Kaelen explains that the blade was blessed by the first oathsworn.

victory.description: The The Dragon’s Dream is resolved successfully

failure.description: The The Dragon’s Dream ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: The dragon is dreaming, and its dreams are manifesting as monsters in the real world. These creatures are twisted reflections of the kingdom’s history and fears.

situation:the-lost-heir.name: The Lost Heir
situation:the-lost-heir.description: Rumors say a child of royal blood survived the initial fall, hiding in the wilds. Some believe this heir is the key to breaking the Dragon’s curse.

risks: Rumors say a child of royal blood survived the initial fall, hiding in the wilds. Some believe this heir is the key to breaking the Dragon’s curse.

actors.agenda: To find the Heir and guide them to the Dragon’s lair.
actors.leverage: Her ability to see the Heir’s magical signature.

defaultAction.now: Walks the Ashen Marches, following a scent only she can smell.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Claims to have found the Heir, but it is a trick to draw people into the forest.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Reveals that the Heir is not human, but a construct of ash and memory.

actors.agenda: To capture the Heir and use them as leverage against Lord Harrow.
actors.leverage: The military might of the remaining guard.

defaultAction.now: Sends scouts into the forests to track any unusual magical signatures.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Arrests anyone who speaks of a "child of ash" as a spy.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Launches a full expedition into the forests, abandoning the city walls.

clock.name: The Bloodline
clock.doom: The Heir is discovered by the Dragon and consumed, ending the royal line forever.
clock.pauseCondition: Players find and protect the Heir before the Dragon does.

stages.name: Rumor
stages.description: Whispers of a child in the woods.

consequences.narrative: Brother Silas claims it is his descendant.

stages.name: Sighting
stages.description: A figure is seen in the trees.

trigger.eventDescription: A scout reports a flash of silver light.

consequences.narrative: Lord Harrow sends assassins.

stages.name: Confrontation
stages.description: Multiple factions converge on the Heir’s location.

consequences.narrative: A skirmish breaks out in the forest.

stages.name: Consumption
stages.description: The Dragon smells the royal blood.

consequences.narrative: The Dragon stirs in its sleep.

outgoingLeads.information: The Heir is not a biological child, but a living memory given form.

discovery.description: Elara reveals that the Heir is made of the same ash as Thalorion.

outgoingLeads.information: Harrow wants the Heir not to rule, but to sacrifice them to the Dragon.

discovery.description: A letter from Harrow to the Cultists details the ritual.

outgoingLeads.information: The Bone White Forests protect the Heir because they are part of the Dragon’s dream.

discovery.description: The trees bend away from the Heir’s camp.

victory.description: The The Lost Heir is resolved successfully

failure.description: The The Lost Heir ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: Rumors say a child of royal blood survived the initial fall, hiding in the wilds. Some believe this heir is the key to breaking the Dragon’s curse.

situation:the-broken-oath.name: The Broken Oath
situation:the-broken-oath.description: One of the great oaths binding the kingdom’s defenses has been broken, causing a magical leak. The source of the break is unknown, but the effects are catastrophic.

risks: One of the great oaths binding the kingdom’s defenses has been broken, causing a magical leak. The source of the break is unknown, but the effects are catastrophic.

actors.agenda: To find the broken oath and renew it, restoring his faith.

defaultAction.now: Prays at the site of the broken oath, trying to feel the magical resonance.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Believes he has found the answer, but misinterprets the sign.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Attempts to renew the oath himself, but lacks the authority, causing a backlash.

actors.agenda: To sing the oath back into existence, using her music to mend the tear.
actors.leverage: Her unique magical affinity for sound and memory.

defaultAction.now: Studies the library’s acoustic properties to find the right frequency.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Attempts a partial song, which stabilizes the leak but drains her voice.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Sings the full oath, healing the leak but erasing her memory of why she loved the King.

openingScenes: The Royal Audience Chamber, suspended in perpetual twilight. The Ashen Sovereign sits upon a throne of petrified ashwood, his form flickering like a candle in a draft. Courtiers bow over empty goblets, reciting titles of provinces that have been dust for decades. Outside, the sky weeps fine gray snow. The party arrives bearing a sealed scroll from a border keep that no longer exists.

The Bridge of Sighs at the city’s edge. A procession of Oathsworn marches toward the dragon’s mountain, their armor polished but their faces hollow. Each warrior carries a glass lantern containing a recorded memory—laughter, a lover’s vow, the taste of rain—to fuel the ward until their next rotation. The bridge groans under the weight of centuries, and somewhere below, something in the mist watches the lanterns burn.

A forgotten waystation deep in the Weeping Marches. The walls are lined with tapestries depicting battles won, but the threads are unraveling into actual ash. A lone lorekeeper sits by a cold hearth, meticulously copying names onto vellum before the ink fades. She looks up as the party approaches, asking not who they are, but what they intend to remember once the wind takes everything.
plotHooks: The Sovereign’s personal cartographer has vanished, taking the last complete map of the eastern provinces. Without it, the court cannot calculate how much ash-tithe the dragon demands next cycle. Find her before the moon wanes, or watch a loyal duchy quietly erased from the tax rolls.

A faction of Dragon-Cultists has begun hoarding memory-crystals in the lower vaults, arguing that preserving the past delays the holy consumption. Sabotage their vaults to restore balance, or ally with them and risk the court declaring you traitors to the realm’s legacy.

Rumors circulate that the ranger-king’s bloodline is failing; his wounds no longer scab over, they simply turn to dust. Send a trusted envoy to the Sunken Sanctum to retrieve a drop of royal essence, knowing the journey may cost more than gold.

A merchant caravan returns from the southern wastes carrying silk woven from dragon-scale, but every thread hums with a faint, hungry resonance. Trade in it to fund your campaigns, or burn it to prove the beast’s appetite can be starved.
commonConflicts: Duty vs. Decay: Characters must choose between maintaining costly traditions that uphold the kingdom’s fragile dignity or abandoning them to conserve resources for survival, often watching elders cling to rituals even as the ground turns to ash beneath their feet.

Memory vs. Oblivion: Power comes from remembering, but clinging too tightly to the past traps people in grief. Parties frequently face dilemmas where preserving a crucial secret or lineage means sacrificing their ability to move forward.

Hope vs. Honesty: The court pretends the empire still thrives to keep the dragon pacified, but common folk know the truth. NPCs and factions constantly negotiate whether to perpetuate the beautiful lie or speak the harsh reality that could fracture morale.
resolutionPatterns: Bittersweet Preservation: The immediate threat is neutralized, but the victory requires a deliberate sacrifice of something irreplaceable—a landmark, a title, a loved one’s name. The party succeeds, but the triumph feels like a farewell.

Graceful Unraveling: Rather than a decisive battle, the conflict resolves through acceptance. An NPC walks willingly into the dragon’s maw, a treaty is signed knowing it will expire, or a relic is deliberately broken to release its stored magic. The ending is quiet, dignified, and irreversible.

Ashen Legacy: The party achieves their goal, but the cost accelerates the realm’s decline. They secure passage, save a province, or break a curse, yet the sky darkens further and the dragon’s patience thins. Survival is purchased with a piece of eternity.
